title: handroanthus caraiba
intro: Handroanthus caraiba is a species of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae, commonly known as the Brazil cherry or cabruca. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine in Brazil and has been found to possess medicinal properties, including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Handroanthus caraiba include its ability to reduce oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antiproliferative effects on cancer cells. Herbal preparations of Handroanthus caraiba typically involve drying and crushing the leaves, which can be steeped in hot water to make a tea or infused in oil to create a topical treatment.
